metadata.notes: agent_supports: yes; daemon_capability: full (recorded TUI /rewind fork with a clean post-rewind arc); driver_capability: ready (keys and wait_turn implemented)

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full (with a parent-link caveat)
driver_capability: ready

## Evidence

**Agent — yes.** The installed `vibe 2.19.1` surface registers `/rewind` as “Rewind to a previous message” (`vibe/cli/commands.py:174-177`). `RewindManager.rewind_to_message` restores checkpointed files when requested and, for its default `inplace=False`, saves the parent then truncates and resets into a fresh session; the interactive TUI path uses that default. This covers both conversation rewind and the file-restoration variant.

**Daemon — full.** The Vibe adapter watches `~/.vibe/logs/session/<session-id>/messages.jsonl` with the directory basename as the session id (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/vibe/agent.go`, `adapter.go`). Its parser maps a user line to working and a terminal assistant line to ready (`parser.go`). The recorded TUI run proves the observable fork: SESSION-1 gets two working→ready turns (events 27885→27890 and 27897→27898); `/rewind` then creates SESSION-2 in ready (27899→27900), and the post-rewind turn takes it working→ready (27912→27913). The same PID reconciliation removes SESSION-1 (27811); that is recording-backed behavior, not a failure of the required new-session arc.

**Driver — ready.** The recipe uses `wait_turn` and raw `keys` for the RewindApp picker. The current driver manifest lists both in `DRIVE_ELICITS`, `wait_turn` polls the completed assistant-turn count, and `keys` sends the supplied tmux key sequence. The existing recording was produced with this recipe.

## Caveat

The fork's `parent_session_id` sidecar field is not parsed into `ParentSessionID`, so irrlicht shows the fork as an independent new session rather than a parent-linked child. The new row and clean post-rewind arc are recorded, so this is a provenance-label caveat, not a daemon bug. ACP's separate `inplace=True` rewind route may rewrite a transcript and is outside this TUI-driver cell; the parser's rotation reset handles the shrink case.

## Observations

This recording's offline replay cannot recover Vibe's mutable meta.json sidecar, so the existing spec intentionally does not assert model, token, or cost observations.
caveats: Parent-link provenance only: meta.json carries parent_session_id on a fork, but the sidecar parser reads working_directory, active_model, and token fields only; nothing sets ParentSessionID. The recorded new row + clean post-rewind arc remain observable, but the linkage label is absent.

SETTLED BY RECORD (2026-07-07, irrlichd 0.5.5+474148d, 7/11 phases): the recording proves the TUI /rewind fork and the post-rewind ready→working→ready arc. It also corrects the old inference that SESSION-1 would linger: events.jsonl emits transcript_removed for SESSION-1 when the same PID reconciles onto SESSION-2.

ACP in-place rewind is outside this TUI /rewind cell. Vibe 2.19.1 sends ACP rewind through inplace=True; the recorded TUI path leaves the default inplace=False and forks. The parser resets its accumulators on a shrink/rotation; a non-shrinking rewrite remains a separate latent boundary (#1104).

sources.note: RewindManager: rewind_to_message truncates the conversation to a user message, optionally restores file snapshots (_restore_checkpoint), then calls reset_session (fork). create_checkpoint per user message.
/rewind slash command registered (alias /rewind, handler _start_rewind_mode); Alt+↑↓/Ctrl+P/N shortcut at :243. Live in vibe 2.19.0.

persist_messages opens messages.jsonl in append mode; save_interaction only appends messages beyond total_messages (never truncates/rewrites); reset_session repoints to a fresh session dir. Proves the append-only tailer is undisturbed by a TUI rewind. Scoped to 2.19.0 as recorded: 2.19.1 adds _overwrite_messages_sync (:273/:290/:411) as an inplace=True-only rewrite arm — see the ACP caveat.
_reset_session mints a NEW session_id (generate_session_id) with parent_session_id = old id — vibe rewind FORKS rather than rewriting in place.
Sidecar parser reads only working_directory/active_model/context_tokens; no parent_session_id / ParentSessionID wiring → fork linkage label dropped (caveat).
Live transcript: meta.json carries parent_session_id (null here, unforked), config.active_model=mistral-medium-3.5, stats.context_tokens=22418. Confirms sidecar fields + model pin.
DRIVE_ELICITS includes keys and wait_turn; wait_turn waits for the expected completed assistant turn and keys emits raw tmux keys. Grounds driver=ready.
Template: the codex fork-variant sibling (daemon=full + linkage caveat). Vibe mirrors its daemon shape and spec phasing.

The 2.19.1 in-place rewrite is gated on inplace=True: the TUI /rewind leaves the default inplace=False (forks; append-only preserved — the path this cell records), and only the ACP loop passes inplace=True. Grounds scoping the daemon premise to the TUI path and treating ACP as an unrecorded boundary.

Fixes the live consequence of an in-place rewrite: the tailer's rotation branch now resets the vibe parser's accumulators via the rotationResetter opt-in (the code comments credit the underlying issue, #1063; #1078 is the PR that closed it) (core/pkg/tailer/tailer.go:569-572, core/adapters/inbound/agents/vibe/parser.go:59-90), so vibe tokens/cost stop flatlining to 0 after a rotation. Regression test TestTokenAccounting_ResumesAfterRotationReset. Why #1089 is a fixture/assessment accuracy matter rather than an open bug.

Scoped this assessment's append-only premise to the TUI /rewind path and documented the ACP inplace=True exception as a deliberate uncovered boundary. agent_cli_version stays 2.19.0 (provenance of the recording, not a claim about the current vibe release); no re-record.

Latent follow-up split out of #1089: an in-place rewrite whose new size is >= the tailer's lastOffset escapes rotation detection (core/pkg/tailer/tailer.go:496-505), so #1078's rotationResetter never fires for it. No adapter triggers it today — claudecode's in-place rewind shrinks the file, and vibe's rewrite arm is ACP-only.

preconditions: mistral-vibe (vibe) v2.19.0+ on PATH and authenticated
irrlichd is recording (isolated run-cell mode, or --attach against a --record daemon)
the interactive driver supports the recipe keys and wait_turn steps
the recording uses the TUI /rewind picker, not Vibe ACP's inplace rewind route
setup: Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/ (vibe mints its own session_<ts>_<hash> id; UUID arg is ignored)

Key sequence matches vibe reality: with no file changes the RewindApp shows a SINGLE option (Edit message from here), so Enter selects it → fork; vibe then pre-fills the input with the rewound message, so C-c clears it before the post-rewind send

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: one
Reply with exactly the word: two
Reply with exactly the word: three

verify: events.jsonl contains a presession_created for proc-<PID> within 1000 ms + pid_discovered
SESSION-1 (session_<ts>_<hash>) appears in ready and runs two clean working→ready arcs (turns one, two)
after /rewind + Enter (single-option RewindApp), a NEW session_<...> dir (SESSION-2) + session row appears (the fork), materializing once the post-rewind turn completes

RECORD-CORRECTED: the original SESSION-1 row IS removed (transcript_removed) as the same PID reconciles onto SESSION-2 — the on-disk messages.jsonl persists, only the daemon session row is dropped (refutes the assessment’s no-removal claim)
post-rewind turn (three) drives a clean ready→working→ready arc on SESSION-2; a benign ~2s post-turn activity blip may follow
final live state is ready; observations: agent-only (model/tokens sidecar-only, unverifiable offline)
